_meta.notes: Deep research session June 10, 2026 — approx. 15 min research across CMS, SHVS, NRHA, RHTP Tracker, KFF, BPC, HRS, RHIhub, state agency websites, and Sellers Dorsey award summaries. KEY UPDATES: (1) Idaho upgraded from pending to ACTIVE — RFP posting confirmed May-June 2026, contracts July-Sept; (2) Indiana has 3 new URGENT RFPs with hard deadlines: MOCC bid due June 15, Preceptor Registry bid due June 15, ACO Feasibility Study due July 6 — CRITICAL; (3) Alaska full application portal open NOW through June 22, 2026 — IMMINENT DEADLINE; (4) Iowa fully active with multiple open RFPs including Combat Cancer Health Hub (open since May 15); (5) Oregon Catalyst Awards closed May 26, awards expected July 2026; (6) Minnesota confirmed $193M award, RFPs coming soon 2026; (7) Connecticut hosting stakeholder webinar June 18; (8) Tennessee first competitive RFPs open July 6-20; (9) Arkansas HOME/THRIVE RPM programs now accepting applications; (10) Vermont RPM NOFO live on Vermont Business Registry; (11) South Carolina Connections to Care RPM program open up to $5M/applicant; (12) Mississippi soliciting Comprehensive State Health Plan RFP (RHTP-aligned); (13) Wisconsin grant writing webinar June 5, still no RHTP-specific RFPs released; (14) Montana confirmed $233M award; (15) New York confirmed $212M budget, CMS approved, applications expected late spring/summer 2026. All state award amounts updated where exact figures were confirmed from state agency or CMS records. All state statuses and deadlines updated. Year 1 funds must be obligated by Oct 30, 2026 (or Sept 30 per some sources) — states are moving fast.

states.name: Alabama
states.agency: Alabama Dept of Economic & Community Affairs (ADECA)
states.deadline: Rolling — check ADECA portal for current RFP windows
states.notes: Alabama RHTP Advisory Group established to guide implementation. ADECA leads administration. RFPs selected via procurement rules. Collaborative EHR and regional IT/cybersecurity hubs among 11 planned initiatives. All funds must be obligated by Oct 30, 2026.

states.name: Alaska
states.agency: Alaska Dept of Health
states.deadline: CRITICAL: Full application portal open June 1–22, 2026 (closes 9:00 AM). Year 2 LOI expected early fall 2026.
states.notes: DEADLINE THIS WEEK: Full application portal closes June 22, 2026 at 9:00 AM. 403 organizations advanced to implementation pathway from first LOI. Plans include drone delivery, remote pharmacy dispensing, and portable diagnostics. All Year 1 funds must be obligated by Dec 31, 2026.

states.name: Arizona
states.agency: Arizona Health Care Cost Containment System (AHCCCS)
states.deadline: Rolling — see AHCCCS procurement portal
states.notes: Revised Budget & Project Narratives publicly available. RHTP Steering Committee active. 4 strategic goals. Community listening sessions completed.

states.name: Arkansas
states.agency: Office of Governor Sarah Huckabee Sanders
states.deadline: Applications open now — HOME initiative (RPM) and THRIVE program accepting applications via apply.arkansasrhtp.com
states.notes: HOME initiative (Health Outcomes Through Monitoring and Engagement) now open — RPM central strategy for chronic disease and post-discharge care. THRIVE program emphasizes continuous patient monitoring. Apply at apply.arkansasrhtp.com.

states.name: California
states.agency: Dept of Health Care Access & Information (HCAI)
states.deadline: See HCAI RHTP page for current application windows
states.notes: Rural Health Policy Council formed. FAQ and timeline published on HCAI website. Texas, Alaska, and California are receiving the three largest total awards nationally.

states.name: Colorado
states.agency: Colorado Dept of Health Care Policy & Financing
states.deadline: RFA open now — webinars June 10, 16, 23, 25, 2026. 'How to Fill Out the RFA' session: June 23, 9:00–10:30 AM. RHTP Advisory Committee: June 22.
states.notes: Awarded $200,105,604. RFA open NOW with multiple webinars in June 2026. 'How to Fill Out the RFA' webinar June 23. Vendor Partnership List published. Partnership Interest Form for grantee collaborations available. Contact: hcpf_rhtp@state.co.us

states.name: Connecticut
states.agency: Connecticut Dept of Social Services
states.deadline: Stakeholder webinar June 18, 2026. See CT DSS procurement portal for rolling RFP opportunities.
states.notes: Governor Lamont announced $154M federal grant. AHEAD-aligned value-based care approach. Stakeholder/public webinar update scheduled June 18, 2026. Implementation underway.

states.name: Delaware
states.agency: Delaware Health and Social Services
states.deadline: Initial RFPs open — Q3 2026 deadlines. Includes plan to establish state's first 4-year medical school.
states.notes: One of the first states to open RFPs. Unique initiative: establishing Delaware's first 4-year medical school with educational awards for ~140 physicians committing to 5-year rural practice. FQHC Value-Based Care Readiness initiative active.

states.name: Florida
states.agency: Florida Agency for Health Care Administration (AHCA)
states.deadline: Rolling via MyFloridaMarketPlace — active now
states.notes: Full application publicly available. Regional map on AHCA RHTP page.

states.name: Georgia
states.agency: Georgia Dept of Community Health
states.deadline: See DCH procurement portal — rolling
states.notes: Known as GREAT Health Program. CMS approved amended first-year budget March 2026. GREAT Health Advisory Council active. AHEAD-aligned value-based care approach. Rural catalyst fund initiative proposed.

states.name: Hawaii
states.agency: Hawaii Dept of Human Services
states.notes: Award confirmed Dec 2025. RHTP Oversight Team and University of Hawaii as initiative leads. Statewide digital backbone (interoperable EHRs) and telehealth network planned. AHEAD-aligned value-based care approach. No RFPs released yet. Pending PSYPACT legislation.

states.name: Idaho
states.agency: Idaho Dept of Health & Welfare
states.deadline: RFP postings and subgrant solicitations began May–June 2026. Contracts executing July–September 2026. All funds obligated by Oct 30, 2026.
states.notes: UPGRADED TO ACTIVE: Budget revision submitted Jan 2026, funding restriction lifted Feb 11, 2026. RFP posting and subgrant solicitations began May–June 2026. Contracts and subgrants executing July–September 2026. 11 initiatives including collaborative EHR, maternal & fetal health, workforce, cancer digital regionalization, mental health. Regional transformation networks leading implementation.

states.name: Illinois
states.agency: Illinois Dept of Healthcare & Family Services
states.deadline: See Illinois Procurement Bulletin — rolling
states.notes: RFPs posted on Illinois Procurement Bulletin. New CHW and Peer Support Specialist (PSS) training and certification program planned. Illinois is a 'Reduced Practice' state per AANP, potentially earning RHTP scoring points for FPA adoption.

states.name: Indiana
states.agency: Indiana Family & Social Services Administration
states.deadline: URGENT: RFP 26-87448 MOCC Operations bid due June 15, 2026 3:00 PM EST. RFP 26-87556 Preceptor Registry bid due June 15, 2026 3:00 PM EST. RFP 26-87667 ACO Feasibility Study bid due July 6, 2026 3:00 PM EST (pre-proposal conference June 11).
states.notes: THREE ACTIVE RFPs WITH HARD DEADLINES THIS WEEK/MONTH: (1) Medical Operations Coordinator Center (MOCC) — bid due June 15. (2) Statewide Preceptor Registry & Stipend Management — bid due June 15. (3) ACO Feasibility Study — pre-proposal June 11, bid due July 6. Plus GROW Regional Grants rolling. Key contact: Mike Huth.

states.name: Iowa
states.agency: Iowa Dept of Health & Human Services
states.deadline: Multiple RFPs open — Combat Cancer Health Hub RFP and Maternal Health Hub RFI open since May 15, 2026. Hometown Connections School-Based Services webinar May 20. Check Iowa HHS Healthy Hometowns funding page.
states.notes: Iowa was FIRST state in nation to award RHTP funding (January 30, 2026). Known as 'Healthy Hometowns.' Multiple rounds already awarded. New: Combat Cancer Health Hub RFP and Maternal Health Hub RFI open as of May 15, 2026. Hometown Connections School-Based Services kickoff webinar May 20.
states.contact: See Iowa HHS Healthy Hometowns page

states.name: Kansas
states.agency: Kansas Dept of Health & Environment
states.deadline: Rolling via KDHE — current windows active
states.notes: Application, approved program docs, and webinar recordings available. Unique initiative: proposes short-term housing in rural communities for up to 100 medical students to support rural recruitment. Full Practice Authority (FPA) for NPs under consideration.

states.name: Kentucky
states.agency: Kentucky Cabinet for Health & Family Services
states.deadline: See CHFS procurement portal — rolling
states.notes: Project Narrative and Supporting Documentation publicly available. 'Rooted in Health, Kentucky Rural Dental Access Program' — expanding rural dental care. EMS and trauma capacity initiative. Emergency crisis care (EmPATH model) included.

states.name: Louisiana
states.agency: Louisiana Dept of Health
states.notes: Award confirmed. Secretary of Louisiana Dept of Health leading implementation. No RFPs released yet. Implementation planning underway per Sellers Dorsey analysis. All Year 1 funds must be obligated by Oct 30, 2026.

states.name: Maine
states.agency: Maine Dept of Health & Human Services
states.deadline: Community RFPs expected Q3 2026 — webinars ongoing. Legislature passed PA scope expansion Feb 2026.
states.notes: Hosting community webinars for clinicians and partners. Budget amended. Maine passed legislation Feb 2026 to expand scope of practice for PAs — strengthens workforce initiative and RHTP scoring. Health career training programs for high school students planned. Formal community RFPs expected Q3 2026.

states.name: Maryland
states.agency: Maryland Dept of Health
states.deadline: RHTP update webinar held April 17, 2026. Funding opportunities anticipated — check MDH RHTP page for release dates.
states.notes: RHTP Project Director under State Office of Rural Health Director (SORH). Funds flow via Immediate Impact awards and competitive Transformation Funds (RFP/RFA). Initiatives include: AHEC expansion, RAMP program, Health Workforce Data Clearinghouse, School-Based Health Centers, CRISP telehealth HIE tools, Food is Medicine initiatives. RFPs anticipated soon.

states.name: Massachusetts
states.agency: Massachusetts Executive Office of Health & Human Services
states.notes: Award confirmed. Facility Modernization and Re-Use initiative planned for hospitals, clinics, and long-term care sites. Telehealth-enabled perinatal care in maternity care deserts. Pending PSYPACT legislation. No RFPs released yet.

states.name: Michigan
states.agency: Michigan Dept of Health & Human Services
states.deadline: See MDHHS procurement portal — rolling
states.notes: Advisory Council applications open. Implementation ramping up. Minnesota RHTP Health IT RFP (INFO ONLY) posted May 26, 2026 — procurement process beginning.

states.name: Minnesota
states.agency: Minnesota Dept of Health
states.deadline: RHTP Health Information Technology Investments RFP (INFO ONLY) posted May 26, 2026. Full funding opportunities to be released in 2026 — subscribe for updates at MDH RHTP page.
states.notes: UPGRADED — Award confirmed at $193,090,618 for Year 1. HIT Investments RFP (INFO ONLY) posted May 26. Pending PSYPACT legislation. Subscribe for updates on MDH RHTP funding page. Full competitive RFPs expected 2026.

states.name: Mississippi
states.agency: Mississippi Division of Medicaid / Mississippi State Dept of Health
states.notes: Award confirmed. Active RFP: Soliciting proposals for Comprehensive State Health Plan with rural health system assessment and RHTP alignment (data-driven health assessment, stakeholder engagement). Health career training programs for high school students planned. No RHTP-specific sub-award RFPs yet.

states.name: Missouri
states.agency: Missouri Dept of Social Services
states.deadline: Multiple RFPs/RFIs rolling Q1–Q3 2026
states.notes: Multi-wave timeline across Q1–Q3 2026. EHR modernization with HIE integration planned. Multi-organizational collaborative approach.

states.name: Montana
states.agency: Montana Dept of Public Health & Human Services
states.deadline: Competitive bidding at bids.mt.gov — rolling. Provider grant applications at ruralhealth.mt.gov. Next Stakeholder Advisory Committee: August 6, 2026 at Montana State University.
states.submission: Portal (bids.mt.gov for vendors; ruralhealth.mt.gov for providers)
states.notes: UPDATED: Awarded $233,509,358 for Year 1. Two separate pathways: vendors bid at bids.mt.gov; providers apply at ruralhealth.mt.gov. Next Stakeholder Advisory Committee meeting August 6, 2026 at Montana State University. Contact: HHSRuralHealthTransformation@mt.gov

states.name: Nebraska
states.agency: Nebraska Dept of Health & Human Services
states.deadline: Open now — rolling since March 2026
states.notes: Soliciting applications since March 2026. Rural catalyst fund initiative planned. EHR HIE integration initiative.

states.name: Nevada
states.agency: Nevada Dept of Health & Human Services / Nevada Health Authority
states.deadline: RHIT Grant RFP open now — Awards: September 2026. Contracts Sept 15, 2026 – Sept 30, 2027. Financial assistance (housing, relocation, signing bonuses, CME stipends) for provider recruitment also available.
states.notes: Rural Health Innovation & Technology (RHIT) Grant RFP released May 2026. Awards September 2026, contracts run Sept 15, 2026 – Sept 30, 2027. Financial assistance for provider housing, relocation, signing bonuses, and CME stipends to attract rural providers.

states.name: New Hampshire
states.agency: NH Dept of Health & Human Services
states.notes: Award confirmed. No RFPs released yet. Governor Kelly Ayotte (Republican, elected 2024) overseeing implementation.

states.name: New Jersey
states.agency: New Jersey Dept of Health
states.deadline: Next RFA round expected Q3 2026 — watch NJ DOH RHTP page
states.notes: UPDATED: Confirmed $147M — lowest award nationally (fewest rural residents). 1st RFA (Technology, Prevention & Workforce Capacity) closed January 20, 2026. Vendor Resource Directory published. Next round expected Q3 2026.

states.name: New Mexico
states.agency: New Mexico Health Care Authority
states.deadline: Multiple RFPs open now — ASO Support RFP and Center for Rural Health Sustainability & Innovation (CRHSI) RFP active. Check NMHCA portal for close dates.
states.notes: Awarded $211,484,740. Active RFPs: Administrative Service Organization (ASO) Support and Center for Rural Health Sustainability & Innovation (CRHSI). 5 initiatives: Rural Health Data Hub, Healthy Horizons, Rooted in New Mexico, Rural Health Innovation Fund, Bridge to Resilience. Passed legislation Feb 2026 to join PSYPACT and other interstate licensure compacts.
states.contact: See New Mexico Health Care Authority RHTP page

states.name: New York
states.agency: New York State Dept of Health
states.deadline: Applications expected late spring/summer 2026 — CMS approved revised $212M budget and 4 initiatives. Funds must be obligated by Oct 30, 2026.
states.notes: UPDATED: CMS approved NY's revised $212M budget and 4 initiatives with little/no change. NYS DOH expecting to make applications available late spring/summer 2026 to meet Oct 30, 2026 obligation deadline. Announced at Rural Health Council meeting. Pending PSYPACT legislation.

states.name: North Carolina
states.agency: NC Dept of Health & Human Services
states.deadline: NC ROOTS Hub Lead contracts finalizing early June 2026. Next competitive RFP opportunities TBD — check NCDHHS RHTP grants page.
states.notes: RHTP codified in state law (H696, April 30, 2026). NC ROOTS Hub Leads selected — contracts finalizing early June 2026. Hubs will conduct regional needs assessments and build local networks across all 100 NC counties. 420+ stakeholders engaged. Serves ~3 million rural North Carolinians. Legislative report due November 29, 2026.

states.name: North Dakota
states.agency: North Dakota Dept of Health & Human Services
states.deadline: 4 active RFPs released ~May 2026 — Q3 2026 deadlines. See ND HHS portal.
states.notes: 4 RFPs released approximately May 2026. Check NRHA tracker and ND HHS portal for exact close dates.
states.contact: See North Dakota HHS RHTP page

states.name: Ohio
states.agency: Ohio Dept of Medicaid
states.deadline: Open now — rolling since March 2026. Remote Patient Monitoring emphasized as high-impact priority.
states.notes: Soliciting applications since March 2026. RPM identified as high-impact investment for chronic disease (diabetes, hypertension, COPD, heart disease) in Ohio's rural communities.

states.name: Oklahoma
states.agency: Oklahoma Health Care Authority
states.deadline: Open now — rolling since March 2026
states.notes: Soliciting applications since March 2026. EHR modernization planned — purchasing/deploying EHR hardware, subsidizing subscriptions, and providing technical assistance for providers to integrate with Oklahoma HIE.

states.name: Oregon
states.agency: Oregon Health Authority
states.deadline: Catalyst Awards RFGP submission period closed May 26, 2026. Award decisions anticipated July 2026. Additional grant opportunities expected.
states.notes: Awarded $197,271,577. Catalyst Awards RFGP closed May 26, 2026 — awards expected July 2026. Next round of RFGP opportunities expected spring/summer 2026. Previous webinar recordings available on OHA RHTP page.

states.name: Pennsylvania
states.agency: PA Dept of Human Services
states.deadline: Active RFPs via PA eMarketplace — RPM initiative up to $5M per applicant currently open.
states.notes: UPDATED portal URL: pa.gov/agencies/dhs RHTP funding opportunities page. RPM program open — up to $5M per applicant as part of $200M+ statewide RHTP investment. Covers RPM devices, staffing, monitoring infrastructure for hypertension, diabetes, COPD, heart disease.

states.name: Rhode Island
states.agency: RI Executive Office of Health & Human Services
states.notes: Award confirmed. NOTE: RI receives the highest funding per rural resident of any state ($6,305 per rural resident) despite having very few rural residents. This makes RI an extremely well-funded program on a per-provider basis when RFPs are released. No RFPs yet.

states.name: South Carolina
states.agency: SC Dept of Health & Human Services / SC Office of Rural Health
states.deadline: Active RFPs open now — 'Connections to Care' RPM initiative up to $5M per applicant. Check SCDHHS and SCORH portals.
states.notes: UPDATED: 'Connections to Care' initiative — up to $5M per applicant for RPM, chronic care management (hypertension, diabetes, COPD, heart disease), and digital infrastructure. Multiple active RFPs. Scoring rubric published May 2026. SCORH also managing RHTP funds.
states.contact: See SC DHHS / SCORH RHTP page

states.name: South Dakota
states.agency: SD Dept of Health (ORHES)
states.deadline: RFP #26-09RHT-022 open now; Round 2 open now. 'Rural Strong Grants' additional opportunity — bid deadline was May 22, 2026 (check for next cycle).
states.notes: Round 1: RFP #26-09RHT-022 — workforce/PM up to $500K Year 1, renewable. Round 2: digital health modernization, doula workforce, caregiver support. 'Rural Strong Grants: Strengthening Access to Care in Rural Communities' — additional procurement opportunity (check SD DOH for next cycle dates).
states.contact: See South Dakota DOH RHTP page

states.name: Tennessee
states.agency: TN Dept of Health
states.deadline: FIRST competitive RFPs releasing July 6, 2026 on TN Dept of General Services. Submission window July 6–20, 2026. Create Partner Portal account NOW to receive notifications.
states.submission: Portal (TN Partner Portal / Dept of General Services)
states.notes: UPDATED: Awarded $206,888,882. FIRST competitive RFPs open July 6–20, 2026 on TN Dept of General Services portal. Partner Portal live NOW — create account to receive notifications and access grant opportunities. Rural catalyst fund initiative planned.
states.contact: See Tennessee DOH RHTP page — register on Partner Portal now

states.name: Texas
states.agency: Texas Health & Human Services Commission
states.deadline: Procurement opportunities via GovDelivery and Electronic State Business Daily. HHSC not meeting with potential vendors to maintain fairness — monitor HHSC Grants Page and subscribe via GovDelivery.
states.notes: UPDATED: 'Rural Texas Strong' — confirmed $281.3M/year, largest award nationally. CMS approved revised budget April 7, 2026. HHSC maintaining vendor-contact fairness restrictions. Consumer-facing health portal and remote monitoring technology initiatives. Must subscribe via GovDelivery for procurement notifications.
states.contact: Subscribe via GovDelivery on HHSC RHTP page

states.name: Utah
states.agency: Utah Dept of Health & Human Services
states.deadline: Legislature approved — active RFPs on DHHS portal
states.notes: Expected ~$500M over 5 years. Legislature approval granted early 2026.
states.contact: Pam Bennett / Sarah Woolsey / Marc Watterson — Utah DHHS

states.name: Vermont
states.agency: Vermont Agency of Human Services
states.deadline: Multiple NOFOs active — RPM NOFO live on Vermont Business Registry (vermontbusinessregistry.com). 14-month initial contracts underway.
states.notes: UPDATED: Active RPM NOFO on Vermont Business Registry — purchasing/leasing RPM technology for home health, primary care, long-term care, and community paramedicine. Supports longitudinal care for aging populations. 14-month initial project; 5-year program.
states.contact: See Vermont AHS RHTP page / Vermont Business Registry

states.name: Virginia
states.agency: Virginia Dept of Medical Assistance Services
states.deadline: See eVA procurement portal — rolling
states.notes: Hiring new staff. RFPs on eVA procurement portal. Dedicated RHTP website at ruralhealthtransformationva.virginia.gov.

states.name: Washington
states.agency: WA Health Care Authority (HCA)
states.deadline: Active RFPs on WEBS now — registration required before viewing/bidding
states.notes: Must register at WEBS (fortress.wa.gov/ga/webs) to view and bid. 6 key initiatives. Led by HCA + DOH + DSHS tri-agency approach.

states.name: West Virginia
states.agency: WV Dept of Health & Human Resources
states.deadline: Active RFPs on DHHR portal now
states.notes: Legislature passed RHTP supplemental appropriation. $199M deployed. Hiring underway. Health career training programs for high school students planned. Ballad Health (WV/TN border) already deploying virtual nursing technology.

states.name: Wisconsin
states.agency: Wisconsin Dept of Health Services
states.notes: Award confirmed. No RHTP-specific RFPs released yet. Free Grant Writing Without Grief webinar held June 5, 2026 for rural WI healthcare staff to prepare for RHTP and other grant opportunities (contact: Kevin Jacobson info@worh.org). Monitor DHS RHTP page.

states.name: Wyoming
states.agency: Wyoming Dept of Health
states.notes: Award confirmed. No RFPs released yet. Governor Mark Gordon (Republican) term-limited in 2026 — gubernatorial transition may affect timeline. Monitor Wyoming DOH RHTP page.

rfpOpportunities.rfp: Year 1 Full Application — Implementation Pathway (CLOSES JUNE 22)
rfpOpportunities.andorNote: AK's geographic challenge makes virtual care essential — ThinkAndor®'s remote monitoring and virtual rounding are critical for Alaska Native and rural Alaskan hospitals. Plans include drone delivery, remote pharmacy dispensing, portable diagnostics.
rfpOpportunities.psynergyNote: Alaska's rural-frontier-tribal care gaps are the exact use case Psynergy was built for. Virtual physicians and APPs serve remote communities that cannot recruit on-site providers.

rfpOpportunities.rfp: RFP 26-87448 — Medical Operations Coordinator Center (MOCC)
rfpOpportunities.status: Bid due June 15, 2026 at 3:00 PM EST — URGENT
rfpOpportunities.andorNote: ThinkAndor®'s AI-powered care coordination and virtual nursing directly map to MOCC's rural care coordination mission.
rfpOpportunities.psynergyNote: Psynergy's virtual clinical coordination model is a direct fit for Indiana's Medical Operations Coordinator Center.

rfpOpportunities.rfp: RFP 26-87556 — Statewide Preceptor Registry & Stipend Management
rfpOpportunities.status: Bid due June 15, 2026 at 3:00 PM EST — URGENT
rfpOpportunities.andorNote: ThinkAndor® can support the technology/platform component of a statewide preceptor management system.
rfpOpportunities.psynergyNote: Psynergy's rural clinical workforce focus maps directly to Indiana's preceptor pipeline and rural training program.

rfpOpportunities.rfp: RFP 26-87667 — ACO Feasibility Study (Medicaid Alternate Payment Model)
rfpOpportunities.status: Pre-proposal conference June 11; Bid due July 6, 2026 at 3:00 PM EST
rfpOpportunities.andorNote: ThinkAndor® can provide technology infrastructure data for ACO feasibility analysis.
rfpOpportunities.psynergyNote: Psynergy's virtual clinical model may support the payment innovation analysis for rural Medicaid.

rfpOpportunities.rfp: RHTP Request for Applications (RFA) — Multiple Initiatives
rfpOpportunities.andorNote: Colorado's technology and value-based care priorities align with ThinkAndor®'s EHR-integrated AI documentation and workflow platform.
rfpOpportunities.psynergyNote: Colorado's workforce initiative is a direct fit for Psynergy's virtual clinical staffing model — augmenting rural clinical teams without requiring local hiring.

rfpOpportunities.rfp: First Competitive RFPs — Multiple Initiatives
rfpOpportunities.status: Open July 6–20, 2026 — register on Partner Portal now
rfpOpportunities.andorNote: Tennessee's rural hospital technology focus aligns with ThinkAndor®'s ambient AI documentation and virtual care platform. Ballad Health (TN/WV) already deployed.
rfpOpportunities.psynergyNote: Psynergy's virtual staffing model directly addresses Tennessee's rural workforce gaps — APPs and virtual RNs can extend capacity without local hiring.

rfpOpportunities.rfp: Healthy Hometowns — Combat Cancer Health Hub RFP + Maternal Health Hub RFI
rfpOpportunities.andorNote: Iowa's Combat Cancer Health Hub could use ThinkAndor®'s remote monitoring and predictive analytics for chronic disease management across rural communities.
rfpOpportunities.psynergyNote: Iowa's Maternal Health Hub and workforce initiatives are a strong fit for Psynergy's virtual clinical staffing — virtual APPs and care coordination for maternal care.
